political.s: Airbnb has a PAC established since 2019 and engages in lobbying activities, particularly around short-term rental regulations. The company has taken positions on election integrity and updated its PAC giving framework in 2021 to withhold support from election objectors. Recent reporting shows aggressive political spending in New York City elections to influence rental policies.
political.amt: $5+ million (NYC SuperPAC 2025); historical PAC donations vary by cycle

charity.s: Airbnb commits $100 million through its Community Fund by 2030, with $36+ million distributed to 70+ countries as of 2024. Donations support environmental sustainability, economic empowerment, ending abuse/exploitation, and disaster relief. Host-driven grantmaking model allows local community input on fund allocation.
charity.focus: Environmental sustainability, economic empowerment, disaster relief, human trafficking prevention, community development

environment.s: Airbnb committed to net-zero operations by 2030 and reduced corporate emissions 17% by 2022 versus baseline. Environmental performance is mixed: strong in corporate emissions reduction and carbon credit programs, but receives poor scores on disaster readiness, water sustainability, and LGBTQ equality in ESG assessments. Hosts encouraged to adopt sustainable practices.

labor.s: Airbnb implements annual pay equity analyses with no unexplained statistically-significant gaps reported as of 2021. Company has tied executive compensation to diversity goals. However, Airbnb hosts are independent contractors without traditional employee protections, raising questions about labor treatment in its gig-economy model. Limited OSHA/NLRB data available.

dei.s: Airbnb has comprehensive DEI commitments with 2025 goals of 20% underrepresented minorities in US workforce and 50% women at all levels globally. Executive compensation tied to diversity goals. Company received Disability Equality Index recognition and launched Project Lighthouse to measure racial disparities on platform. Strong internal DEI infrastructure with employee resource groups.

animals.s: Not applicable. Airbnb is an online accommodation platform with no animal testing or animal products component to its business model.

guns.s: Not applicable. Airbnb is an online accommodation platform that does not manufacture, sell, or distribute firearms.

privacy.s: Airbnb states it does not sell personal data and implements encryption, multi-factor authentication, and data access rights for users. Privacy policy emphasizes transparency and user control. However, the company shares data with third-party service providers, building management, and uses biometric data (facial recognition) for verification. Data access procedures are available but differ by jurisdiction.

execPay.s: CEO Brian Chesky receives nominal $1 base salary with compensation heavily equity-based and performance-tied. Other executives earned $8.8-18.8 million in 2024, largely from stock awards. Executive compensation is tied to diversity metrics and stakeholder priorities. Pay structure emphasizes long-term equity incentives over cash compensation.
execPay.ratio: Not disclosed (CEO takes $1 salary; equity-based compensation prevents traditional ratio)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
